title: Vehicle repair material prediction and verification system
abstract: A method includes determining 306, based on current repair data and at least one of historical repair data or existing repair specifications, a predicted material to be used during a vehicle repair, the vehicle repair including replacing or repairing a part of a vehicle. The material includes at least one of an adhesive, an abrasive, a tape, a paint, a coating, or a tool. The method also includes outputting 308 data indicating the predicted material in a predicted material repair plan.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A computing system comprising at least one processor configured to: determine, based on current repair data and at least one of historical data or an existing repair specification, a predicted material to be used during a vehicle repair, the vehicle repair including replacing or repairing a part of a vehicle, wherein the predicted material includes at least one of an adhesive, an abrasive, a tape, a paint, a coating, or a tool; and output data indicating the predicted material in a predicted material repair (PMR) plan . 2. The computing system of claim 1, wherein the at least one processor is further configured to: receive data indicating an actual material used during the vehicle repair; determine whether the predicted material includes the actual material; and output data indicating whether the vehicle repair was performed according to the PMR plan. 3. The computing system of claim 1 or 2, wherein the at least one processor is further configured to: determine a manufacturer specified material to be used during the vehicle repair; determine whether the predicted material to be used includes the manufacturer specified material; and output data indicating whether the predicted material to be used includes the manufacturer specified material. 4. The computing system of any one of claims 1-3, wherein the current repair data includes data indicating a type of the vehicle repair. 5. The computing system of any one of claims 1-4, wherein the current repair data includes data indicating a trim level of the vehicle. 6. The computing system of any one of claims 1-5, wherein the current repair data includes data indicating a vehicle identification number of the vehicle. 7. The computing system of any one of claims 1-6, wherein the current repair data includes data indicating a mileage of the vehicle or a location of the vehicle. 8. The computing system of any one of claims 1-7, wherein a model year of the vehicle is a new model year, wherein the historical repair data includes vehicle repair data for vehicle repairs of previous model years of a same type as a type of the vehicle and does not include vehicle repair data for vehicle repairs of the new model year of the type of the vehicle. 9. The computing system of any one of claims 1-8, wherein the at least one processor is further configured to generate an order for additional materials based at least in part on the predicted material to be used during the vehicle repair. 10. The computing system of any one of claims 1-9, wherein the at least one processor is further configured to: determine a predicted quantity of the predicted material to be used during the vehicle repair based on the current repair data and at least one of the historical data or the existing repair specification; and output data indicating the predicted quantity of the predicted material in the PMR plan. 11. The computing system of claim 10, wherein the at least one processor is further configured to: receive data indicating an actual quantity of an actual material used during the vehicle repair; and determine whether the actual quantity of the actual material is within a threshold of the predicted quantity of the PMR plan. 12. The computing system of claim 10 or 11, wherein the at least one processor is configured to determine the predicted quantity of the predicted material based on historical repair data associated with an entity performing the vehicle repair, and wherein the at least one processor is further configured to: determine, based on historical repair data associated with a plurality of entities, an average quantity of material used by the plurality of entities during vehicle repairs of a same type as a type of the vehicle repair; and output data indicating the predicted quantity of the predicted material to be used by the entity and the average quantity of the material used by the plurality of entities. 13. The computing system of any one of claims 10-12, wherein the at least one processor is further configured to determine the predicted quantity of the predicted material to be used during the vehicle repair further based on at least one of: the type of the vehicle repair, the trim level of the vehicle, the vehicle identification number of the vehicle, the mileage of the vehicle, the location of the vehicle or a physical measurement of the part to be repaired or replaced. 14. A method comprising: determining, based on current repair data and at least one of historical data or an existing repair specification, a predicted material to be used during a vehicle repair, the vehicle repair including replacing or repairing a part of a vehicle, wherein the predicted material includes at least one of an adhesive, an abrasive, a tape, a paint, a coating, or a tool; and outputting data indicating the predicted material in a predicted material repair (PMR) plan. 15. The method of claim 14, further comprising: receiving data indicating an actual material used during the vehicle repair; determining whether the actual material includes the predicted material; and output data indicating whether the vehicle repair was performed according to the PMR plan. 16. The method of any one of claims 14-15, further comprising: determining a manufacturer specified material to be used during the vehicle repair; determining whether the predicted material to be used includes the manufacturer specified material; and outputting data indicating whether the predicted material to be used includes the manufacturer specified material. 17. The method of any one of claims 14-16, wherein the current repair data includes data indicating at least one of a type of the vehicle repair, a trim level of the vehicle, a vehicle identification number of the vehicle, a mileage of the vehicle, and a location of the vehicle. 18. The method of any one of claims 14-17, further comprising: 

 determine a predicted quantity of the predicted material to be used during the vehicle repair based on the current repair data and at least one of the historical data or the existing repair specification; and output data indicating the predicted quantity of the predicted material. 19. The method of claim 18, further comprising: receiving data indicating an actual quantity of an actual material used during the vehicle repair; and determining whether the actual quantity of the actual material is within a threshold of a quantity defined by the PMR plan. 20. A computer-readable storage medium comprising instructions that, when executed, cause at least one processor of a computing device to: determine, based on current repair data and at least one of historical data or an existing repair specification, a predicted material to be used during a vehicle repair, the vehicle repair including replacing or repairing a part of a vehicle, wherein the predicted material includes at least one of an adhesive, an abrasive, a tape, a paint, a coating, or a tool; and output data indicating the predicted material in a PMR plan.
